title: Para-Summary
learning_outcomes: Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Condensing multi-sentence paragraphs into a single core thesis statement.

content_sections.title: Para-Summary
content_sections.content:
### Syntactic Alignment, Modifiers & Logical Transition Indicators
Verbal Aptitude studies grammatical structure, modifier bounds, and logical flow connections.

#### Subject-Verb Agreement with Intervening Phrases
Verbs must align with their subjects, not object nouns located in intervening prepositional phrases (e.g. "One of the servers *is* down," not "One of the servers *are* down").

#### Dangling Modifiers
A modifying phrase must refer to an active subject declared immediately after the phrase. If the subject is omitted, the modifier is left dangling:
*   *Dangling (Incorrect):* "While writing code, the system crashed."
*   *Resolved (Correct):* "While I was writing code, the system crashed."

#### Logical Transition categories
*   **Adversative:** Indicates contrast (e.g. *nevertheless*, *nonetheless*, *conversely*).
*   **Causal:** Indicates result (e.g. *therefore*, *hence*, *consequently*).

### Depth Analysis: Para-Summary
In production configurations, we analyze how Condensing multi-sentence paragraphs into a single core thesis statement affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Para-Summary
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Para-Summary is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Para-Summary
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Para-Summary loops.
